executive_summary: The iteration is functionally complete as a scaffold. All five user stories and six of seven functional requirements fully comply. FR-2 partially complies: the implementation is a deliberate stdlib stub with no diffusers/torch dependency. The refactor plan targets real inference integration as Priority 1.
minor_observations: No dedicated test_us001_* test methods; US-001 covered implicitly only.
--steps only affects the stub hash color, not real inference fidelity.
Top-level exception handler is pragma: no cover; a mock test would close the gap.
HF_REPO_ID_PATTERN is permissive; needs tightening when real model loading is introduced.
seed serialises as JSON null when not provided; downstream consumers must handle null vs. integer.
conclusions_and_recommendations: Replace the stdlib stub with a real diffusers + torch pipeline in the next iteration, preserving the existing CLI contract, JSON schema, prompt enhancement logic, and exit-code semantics. Secondary: add explicit US-001 tests, cover the top-level error handler, document null-seed behaviour, and tighten HF_REPO_ID_PATTERN.

priority_1_real_inference.goal: Replace write_png stub with actual diffusers + torch pipeline (FR-2 gap).
steps: Add diffusers, torch, accelerate to requirements.txt / pyproject.toml.
Extract current stub into stub_backend.py for use in unit tests without torch.
Implement diffusers_backend.py: generate_image(enhanced_prompt, pipeline_config, steps, seed) -> PIL.Image.
Swap in real backend in run(); keep stub as fallback/test shim.
Guard diffusers import with try/except; emit clear error if dependencies missing.

priority_2_test_hardening.action: Mock write_png to raise; assert exit code 1 and stderr content.
Document that seed field is null in JSON when not supplied.

refactor_plan.priority_3_input_validation: Tighten HF_REPO_ID_PATTERN to enforce HuggingFace naming constraints (max 96 chars per segment, no leading/trailing hyphens) once real model loading is in place.
